__description: The power module provides access to the Windows power management
__tinydesc: Provides an interface to the Windows power management

battery.__description:
The power.battery property gets the current battery usage as a table. If no battery is present, the property returns nil.

The table provides the following fields :

  • charging : indicates if the battery is charging or not.
  • percent : indicates the battery left, in percent.
  • lifetime : indicates the battery lifetime in seconds or nil if the system cannot calculate it.
  • saver : indicates if the battery saver is currently in use.
battery.__example:
local power = require "power"

if power.battery then
  print("Battery is "..(power.battery.charging and "charging" or "not charging"))
  print(power.battery.percent.."% left")
  print(power.battery.lifetime and math.floor(power.battery.lifetime/3600).."h remaining" or "Battery lifetime not available")
  print(power.battery.saver and "Battery saver is in use" or "Battery saver is not in use")
else
  print("This computer don't have a battery")
end

plugged.__description: The power.plugged checks if the computer is plugged to AC, returning true or false otherwise.
plugged.__example:
local power = require "power"

print("Computer is currently powered by "..(power.isplugged and "AC" or "battery"))
plugged.__tinydesc: Check if the computer is plugged to AC

onPlugged.__description: The power.onPlugged() event is thrown each time the computer is plugged or unplugged from AC.
onPlugged.__tinydesc: Event fired when the power source has changed

__params.__description: A string representing new source of power, either 'battery' or 'AC' otherwise.

onPlugged.__example:
local power = require "power"
function power.onPlugged(source)
  print("Computer is now "..source.." powered")
end
waitall()

onSuspend.__description:
The power.onSuspend() event is thrown each time the computer is going to be suspended.

Notes

  • On modern computers (using S0 power state), this event may not fire correctly.
onSuspend.__tinydesc: Event fired when computer is going to be suspended
onSuspend.__example:
local power = require "power"

function power.onSuspend()
  print("Computer is suspending now !!!")
end

print("Waiting for the computer to suspend...")

waitall()

onResume.__description: The power.onResume() event is thrown each time the computer is resumed from sleep.
onResume.__tinydesc: Event fired when computer is resumed
onResume.__example:
local power = require "power"

function power.onResume()
  print("Computer just resumed from sleep state")
end

print("Waiting for the computer to resume...")

waitall()

onBatteryLow.__description: The power.onBatteryLow() event is thrown when the battery is low.
onBatteryLow.__tinydesc: Event fired when battery is low
onBatteryLow.__example:
local power = require "power"

function power.onBatteryLow()
  print("Battery is low, plug your computer to AC source !")
end

waitall()

hold.__returns: This function returns true on success or false otherwise.
hold.__description:
This function prevents the computer from going into sleep mode. Call power.unhold() to permit sleep mode again.

Notes

  • This function does not block user-initiated sleep: If the user closes the lid, presses the power button, or selects Sleep manually, the system will still sleep.
  • On newer devices (especially laptops), the request may be ignored or auto-terminated after 5 minutes on battery power.
hold.__tinydesc: Prevents the computer from going into sleep mode
hold.__example:
local power = require "power"

print("Preventing the computer to go to sleep mode for 5sec")
print(power.hold())

sleep(5000)

print("Computer can now go to sleep")
print(power.unhold())

unhold.__returns: This function returns true on success or false otherwise.
unhold.__description: This function permits the computer to enter sleep mode again, after a previous call to power.hold().
unhold.__tinydesc: Permits the computer to go to sleep
unhold.__example:
local power = require "power"

print("Preventing the computer to go to sleep mode for 5sec")
print(power.hold())

sleep(5000)

print("Computer can now go to sleep")
print(power.unhold())

hibernate.__returns: This function returns true on success or false otherwise.
hibernate.__description:
power.hibernate() suspends the computer in hibernation mode.

Notes

  • Hibernation mode must have been activated in the Windows settings for this function to work.
hibernate.__tinydesc: Suspends the computer in hibernation mode
hibernate.__example:
local power = require "power"

print("Computer will hibernate in 5sec !")

sleep(5000)

power.hibernate()

sleep.__returns: This function returns true on success or false otherwise.
sleep.__description:
power.hibernate() suspends the computer in sleep mode.

Notes

  • On modern computers using the S0 power state, this function may fail.
sleep.__tinydesc: Suspends the computer in sleep mode
sleep.__example:
local power = require "power"

print("Computer will sleep in 5sec !")

sleep(5000)

power.sleep()

displayoff.__example:
local power = require "power"

print("Monitor will turned off in 5sec !")

sleep(5000)

power.displayoff()
